title: Generalize the rule engine beyond Monaco with legality-debt and mandated-stop features
summary: Official rules create deterministic pressure to stop that simple tyre-window heuristics do not fully encode: standard races require two dry specs unless wet/intermediate are used, Monaco 2025 added a mandatory two-stop / three-set rule, and Qatar 2023 had an FIA/Pirelli maximum stint cap.
rationale: Official rules create deterministic pressure to stop that simple tyre-window heuristics do not fully encode: standard races require two dry specs unless wet/intermediate are used, Monaco 2025 added a mandatory two-stop / three-set rule, and Qatar 2023 had an FIA/Pirelli maximum stint cap. One prior node touched Monaco, but not a full rule-state engine.
implementation_hint: Create causal state features such as slick-compounds-used-so-far, mandatory-stop-debt, minimum-stops-remaining, rule-satisfied flag, special-race max-stint breach risk, and remaining legal paths to finish. Apply only to the correct Year x Race regimes with explicit rule tables.
expected_effect: Should sharpen probability spikes in special-rule races and reduce confusion where the model currently treats all circuits as standard one/two-stop strategy problems.
risk: Small support per special regime means easy overfitting; rule tables must be exact by year and wet/dry exemption.
